Plan Only
1 Test Case Plan
TRUNK-01 CLI help + version
P0 #
R-IDs: R-01
General
Steps
  1. `node bin/trunknux.mjs --version` and `node bin/trunknux.mjs --help` are executed.
Pre-condition (Given)
Node.js >= 20 and the trunknux binary are available.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Both exit 0.
  • ✓--version emits a semver string.
  • ✓--help lists all top-level commands including init, report, validate, demo, doctor, rtm, sca, discover, plan, codify, enrich, batch-plan, br, sign, run, compare, visual.
TRUNK-02 init: scaffold folder structure
P0 #
R-IDs: R-01
General
Steps
  1. `trunknux init test-pass-a --industry general --out ` is executed.
Pre-condition (Given)
A clean temporary directory. `--industry general` and `--out ` are provided.
Expected outcome
  • ✓A folder named `_test-pass-a` is created.
  • ✓test-plan.md, spec.ts, README.md, and evidence/ are all present inside it.
TRUNK-03 init: idempotency
P1 #
R-IDs: R-01
File Handling
Steps
  1. The same init command is run a second time.
Pre-condition (Given)
init has already been run once for a given slug.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Exit code 0 on both runs.
  • ✓Exactly one folder matching the slug exists (no duplicates).
TRUNK-04 init: unknown industry value
P1 #
R-IDs: R-01
General
Steps
  1. `trunknux init bad-industry-test --industry unknownindustry999 --out ` is executed.
Pre-condition (Given)
An unrecognized industry string is passed (e.g. "unknownindustry999").
Expected outcome
  • ✓Process exits with code 0, 1, or 2.
  • ✓No uncaught TypeError or stack trace appears in stderr.
TRUNK-05 validate: missing folder exits 2
P0 #
R-IDs: R-03
General
Steps
  1. `trunknux validate ` is executed.
Pre-condition (Given)
A path that does not exist on disk.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Exit code 2.
  • ✓stderr contains a message matching /not found|missing|does-not-exist/i.
TRUNK-06 validate: valid test-plan.md exits 0
P0 #
R-IDs: R-03
General
Steps
  1. `trunknux validate ` is executed.
Pre-condition (Given)
A folder containing a test-plan.md with valid frontmatter (status: READY, valid r_ids array, valid tc_prefix).
Expected outcome
  • ✓Exit code 0.
TRUNK-07 validate: malformed frontmatter exits 3
P0 #
R-IDs: R-03
Auth — Validation
Steps
  1. `trunknux validate ` is executed.
Pre-condition (Given)
A test-plan.md with an invalid status value and non-array r_ids.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Exit code 3 (parse/validation error).
TRUNK-08 validate: --json mode
P1 #
R-IDs: R-03 R-37
General
Steps
  1. `trunknux --json validate ` is executed.
Pre-condition (Given)
A valid test-plan.md folder.
Expected outcome
  • ✓stdout contains at least one line of valid JSON.
  • ✓Parsed JSON has an "event" key.
TRUNK-09 doctor: runs without crashing
P0 #
R-IDs: R-05
General
Steps
  1. `trunknux doctor` is executed.
Pre-condition (Given)
Standard CI environment with Node >= 20.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Exit code 0 or 1.
  • ✓No uncaught TypeError or "Cannot read properties" in stderr.
TRUNK-10 doctor: --json output shape
P1 #
R-IDs: R-05 R-37
General
Steps
  1. `trunknux doctor --json` is executed.
Pre-condition (Given)
Standard environment.
Expected outcome
  • ✓First JSON line has event "doctor.result", a "checks" array, and a "passed" boolean.
TRUNK-11 report: missing test-plan.md exits 1
P0 #
R-IDs: R-02
General
Steps
  1. `trunknux report ` is executed.
Pre-condition (Given)
A folder with no test-plan.md.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Exit code 1.
  • ✓Combined stdout/stderr matches /test-plan\.md|not found|missing/i.
TRUNK-12 report: generates XLSX + HTML
P0 #
R-IDs: R-02
File Handling
Steps
  1. `trunknux report --plan-only` is executed.
Pre-condition (Given)
A folder with a valid test-plan.md (SMK TC matrix, standard frontmatter).
Expected outcome
  • ✓Exit code 0.
  • ✓A .xlsx file and a .html file appear in the folder.
TRUNK-13 rtm: missing requirements/ exits 2
P0 #
R-IDs: R-07
General
Steps
  1. `trunknux rtm --dry-run` is executed.
Pre-condition (Given)
A directory with no requirements/ folder.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Exit code 2.
  • ✓stderr matches /requirements|not found|REQUIREMENTS\.md/i.
TRUNK-14 rtm: --dry-run prints RTM to stdout
P0 #
R-IDs: R-07
General
Steps
  1. `trunknux rtm --dry-run` is executed.
Pre-condition (Given)
A requirements/REQUIREMENTS.md with at least two R-ID entries.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Exit code 0.
  • ✓stdout contains "Traceability", "R-01", and "R-02".
TRUNK-15 global --json flag: valid JSON on failure
P1 #
R-IDs: R-37
General
Steps
  1. `trunknux --json validate ` is executed.
Pre-condition (Given)
A folder with no markdown files (will produce a validation warning/error).
Expected outcome
  • ✓Combined stdout+stderr contains at least one parseable JSON line with an "event" key.
TRUNK-16 malaysia-banking: bundle file structure
P1 #
R-IDs: R-44
File Handling
Steps
  1. File is read and JSON.parse() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
src/config/industry-standards/malaysia-banking.json exists.
Expected outcome
  • ✓No parse error.
  • ✓Config has industry: "malaysia-banking", version: "0.2.0", and a non-empty standards array.
TRUNK-17 malaysia-banking: control schema completeness
P1 #
R-IDs: R-44
General
Steps
  1. Every entry in standards[] is inspected.
Pre-condition (Given)
The malaysia-banking.json config is loaded.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Each entry has id, name, description, family, and references (array).
TRUNK-18 malaysia-banking: PDPA/BNM/CSA counts
P1 #
R-IDs: R-44
General
Steps
  1. Standards are filtered by id prefix.
Pre-condition (Given)
The malaysia-banking.json config is loaded.
Expected outcome
  • ✓>= 8 PDPA, >= 12 BNM, >= 3 CSA, >= 25 total.
TRUNK-19 malaysia-banking: no proprietary references
P0 #
R-IDs: R-57
File Handling
Steps
  1. File content is searched for the project's reserved-strings list (the names of upstream proprietary projects that must not appear in OSS — see R-QA-06 for the canonical list, kept only inside the test source itself).
Pre-condition (Given)
The malaysia-banking.json config file is read as raw string.
Expected outcome
  • ✓No reserved string is found in the bundle file.
TRUNK-20 report: parsers — parseTestPlanContent
P0 #
R-IDs: R-02 R-49
General
Steps
  1. parseTestPlanContent() is called with the string.
Pre-condition (Given)
A valid test-plan.md string with frontmatter and TC matrix rows.
Expected outcome
  • ✓TC matrix rows and frontmatter fields are correctly extracted.
TRUNK-21 report: parsers — parseExecutionLogContent
P0 #
R-IDs: R-02
Dashboard
Steps
  1. parseExecutionLogContent() and normalizeStatus() are called.
Pre-condition (Given)
An execution-log.md string with TC ID / Status / Result Notes rows.
Expected outcome
  • ✓TC status values (PASS, FAIL, BLOCKED, SKIPPED) parsed and normalized correctly.
TRUNK-22 report: XLSX renderer
P0 #
R-IDs: R-02 R-46
General
Steps
  1. buildXlsx() is called and written to disk.
Pre-condition (Given)
A parsed TC list and execution results.
Expected outcome
  • ✓A valid ExcelJS workbook is produced.
  • ✓Opening the file with ExcelJS succeeds.
TRUNK-23 report: HTML renderer
P0 #
R-IDs: R-02 R-51
General
Steps
  1. buildHtml() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
A parsed TC list with standards alignment.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Returned string is valid HTML.
  • ✓Contains no external HTTP href or src references.
TRUNK-24 report: plan-only mode
P0 #
R-IDs: R-36
General
Steps
  1. `trunknux report --plan-only` is executed.
Pre-condition (Given)
A folder with test-plan.md but no execution-log.md.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Exit code 0.
  • ✓Report renders with "PLAN ONLY" badge.
  • ✓Execution result columns absent or empty.
TRUNK-25 sign-pdf: HMAC badge in PDF
P0 #
R-IDs: R-16 R-31
General
Steps
  1. runSignPdf() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
A surface folder with a valid uat-log.jsonl (chain intact). puppeteer-core is mocked.
Expected outcome
  • ✓mockSetContent receives HTML containing "CHAIN VALID" badge.
  • ✓mockPdf is called.
TRUNK-26 sign-pdf: broken chain badge
P1 #
R-IDs: R-16 R-31
General
Steps
  1. runSignPdf() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
A surface folder with a tampered uat-log.jsonl (chain broken).
Expected outcome
  • ✓PDF renders successfully.
  • ✓HTML contains "CHAIN BROKEN" banner.
  • ✓No abort.
TRUNK-27 sign-pdf: Chrome path env respected
P1 #
R-IDs: R-10 R-16
General
Steps
  1. runSignPdf() is invoked.
Pre-condition (Given)
CHROME_PATH env var is set to a specific path.
Expected outcome
  • ✓mockLaunch is called with executablePath matching the CHROME_PATH value.
TRUNK-28 uat-log: append + chain integrity
P0 #
R-IDs: R-15 R-30
General
Steps
  1. appendEntry() is called with ENTRY_A, ENTRY_B, ENTRY_C in sequence.
Pre-condition (Given)
A fresh temp directory with UAT_SECRET set.
Expected outcome
  • ✓uat-log.jsonl contains 3 lines.
  • ✓Each line is valid JSON with prev_hash and hmac fields.
TRUNK-29 uat-log: verifyChain passes valid chain
P0 #
R-IDs: R-15 R-30
Auth — Happy Path
Steps
  1. verifyChain() is called with the correct secret.
Pre-condition (Given)
A valid uat-log.jsonl with 3 unmodified entries.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Returns { valid: true } (or equivalent success indicator).
  • ✓Exit 0.
TRUNK-30 uat-log: tamper detection — mutation
P0 #
R-IDs: R-15 R-30
General
Steps
  1. verifyChain() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
A valid uat-log.jsonl. Entry B's status field is overwritten to a different value.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Chain reports invalid.
  • ✓The broken-at line is deterministic (entry B's line).
TRUNK-31 uat-log: tamper detection — deletion
P0 #
R-IDs: R-15 R-30
General
Steps
  1. verifyChain() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
A valid uat-log.jsonl. Entry B is deleted (gap in chain).
Expected outcome
  • ✓Chain reports invalid.
TRUNK-32 uat-log: tamper detection — reorder
P0 #
R-IDs: R-15 R-30
General
Steps
  1. verifyChain() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
A valid uat-log.jsonl. Entries A and B are swapped.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Chain reports invalid.
TRUNK-33 uat-log: wrong secret fails verify
P0 #
R-IDs: R-15 R-30
Auth — Validation
Steps
  1. verifyChain() is called with WRONG_SECRET.
Pre-condition (Given)
A valid uat-log.jsonl built with SECRET. WRONG_SECRET is used for verification.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Chain reports invalid.
TRUNK-34 uat-log: justify-with-llm fallback
P1 #
R-IDs: R-18
General
Steps
  1. sign command is invoked with --justify-with-llm.
Pre-condition (Given)
CLAUDE_API_KEY is not set in the environment.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Command continues normally.
  • ✓No error thrown.
  • ✓Brief notice about missing key (optional).
TRUNK-35 sign-stale: parseThreshold parses duration strings
P1 #
R-IDs: R-17
General
Steps
  1. parseThreshold() is called with each string.
Pre-condition (Given)
Duration strings "7d", "30d", "90d", "180d", "365d".
Expected outcome
  • ✓Returns correct millisecond value (e.g.
  • ✓7d = 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000).
TRUNK-36 sign-stale: stale entry detection
P0 #
R-IDs: R-17
General
Steps
  1. runSignStaleCheck() is called with a threshold of (e.g.) 90 days.
Pre-condition (Given)
A uat-log.jsonl with entries whose timestamps are artificially set > threshold days ago.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Stale entries are reported.
  • ✓Non-stale runs produce "no stale entries".
TRUNK-37 oscal: toOSCAL returns assessment-results
P0 #
R-IDs: R-11 R-45
General
Steps
  1. toOSCAL(buildSCA()) is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
A minimal SCA object with 3 controls, 2 evidence items, 1 declined control, 1 sign-off.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Returns an object with a top-level "assessment-results" key of type object.
TRUNK-38 oscal: OSCAL structure — UUID and dates
P0 #
R-IDs: R-11 R-45
General
Steps
  1. UUID and date fields are inspected with regex validators.
Pre-condition (Given)
OSCAL document produced by toOSCAL().
Expected outcome
  • ✓All UUIDs match loose UUID regex.
  • ✓Published date matches ISO-8601.
TRUNK-39 oscal: validateOSCAL rejects malformed doc
P0 #
R-IDs: R-11 R-45
Auth — Validation
Steps
  1. validateOSCAL() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
An OSCAL document missing required fields (e.g. no assessment-results key).
Expected outcome
  • ✓OscalValidationError is thrown with a structured message.
TRUNK-40 oscal: assessment-log extension merge
P1 #
R-IDs: R-11 R-49
General
Steps
  1. mergeAssessmentLog() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
An OSCAL document and a set of uat-log entries.
Expected outcome
  • ✓assessment-log section populated with the uat-log entry records.
TRUNK-41 parser: parseRequirements happy path
P0 #
R-IDs: R-07
Auth — Happy Path
Steps
  1. parseRequirements() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
A markdown string with ## R-01 Title and ## R-02 — Title (em-dash) headings.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Returns array with correct id, title, status for each entry.
TRUNK-42 parser: parseRequirements edge cases
P1 #
R-IDs: R-07
Dashboard
Steps
  1. parseRequirements() is called with each input.
Pre-condition (Given)
Various edge inputs: null, undefined, BLOCKED/PARTIAL/DEFERRED statuses, missing status, single-hash heading, four-hash heading.
Expected outcome
  • ✓null/undefined returns [].
  • ✓BLOCKED/PARTIAL/DEFERRED statuses parsed.
  • ✓Missing status defaults to UNKNOWN.
  • ✓Four-hash headings not parsed.
TRUNK-43 br-attestations: appendAttestation + chain
P0 #
R-IDs: R-22
Dashboard
Steps (2)
  1. appendAttestation() called with ATTEST_QA and
  2. ATTEST_COMPLIANCE.
Pre-condition (Given)
A clean temp dir. UAT_SECRET set.
Expected outcome
  • ✓br-attestations.jsonl has 2 entries.
  • ✓Each has br_id, reviewer, role, timestamp, prev_hash, hmac.
TRUNK-44 br-attestations: revokeAttestation
P1 #
R-IDs: R-22
Dashboard
Steps
  1. revokeAttestation() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
A br-attestations.jsonl with an existing attestation.
Expected outcome
  • ✓A new revocation entry is appended.
  • ✓Prior entries are not deleted or modified.
TRUNK-45 br-attestations: N-of-M attestation status
P0 #
R-IDs: R-22
Dashboard
Steps
  1. getAttestationStatus("BR-01") is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
Two distinct reviewer entries for BR-01 in the log.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Returns an object reflecting 2 distinct reviewers and their attestation state.
TRUNK-46 br-attestations: chain tamper detection
P0 #
R-IDs: R-22 R-30
Dashboard
Steps
  1. verifyAttestationChain() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
A valid br-attestations.jsonl. One entry's justification field is mutated.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Chain verification returns invalid.

P1 #
R-IDs: R-21
Responsive / Mobile
Steps
  1. parseViewport() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
Viewport strings in "1280x800" and "1920X1080" formats.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Returns { width, height } objects with correct numeric values.

P1 #
R-IDs: R-21
General
Steps
  1. resolveUrl() and parseUrlsFlag() are called.
Pre-condition (Given)
URL flag strings in "TC-ID=https://example.com" format.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Correct URL map is produced.
  • ✓TC-ID keys map to URL strings.
TRUNK-49 visual: decideDiffStatus
P1 #
R-IDs: R-22
Dashboard
Steps
  1. decideDiffStatus() is called with each percentage.
Pre-condition (Given)
Pixel-change percentages and a threshold value.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Returns PASS when below threshold, FAIL when above.
TRUNK-50 plan: LLM-assisted plan generation with [VERIFY] markers
P0 #
R-IDs: R-24 R-29
General
Steps
  1. runPlan() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
A scenarios.md file in the test-pass folder. Anthropic API mocked to return TC rows.
Expected outcome
  • ✓test-plan.md written with valid frontmatter.
  • ✓Every LLM-generated TC row contains [VERIFY].
TRUNK-51 plan: --dry-run cost estimate
P1 #
R-IDs: R-24 R-35
General
Steps
  1. runPlan() called with dryRun: true.
Pre-condition (Given)
A scenarios.md and Anthropic API mocked.
Expected outcome
  • ✓No file written.
  • ✓Token count and estimated cost printed to stdout.
  • ✓API mock not called.
TRUNK-52 discover: LLM scenario generation
P0 #
R-IDs: R-23
General
Steps
  1. runDiscover() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
A URL is provided. Page HTML mocked. Anthropic API returns Given/When/Then scenarios.
Expected outcome
  • ✓scenarios.md written with structured Given/When/Then blocks.
TRUNK-53 discover: --dry-run mode
P1 #
R-IDs: R-23 R-35
General
Steps
  1. runDiscover() called with dryRun: true.
Pre-condition (Given)
A URL provided. dryRun: true.
Expected outcome
  • ✓No scenarios.md written.
  • ✓Prompt text and cost estimate printed.
  • ✓API mock not called.
TRUNK-54 codify: generates spec with requestSubmit + afterEach
P0 #
R-IDs: R-25
General
Steps
  1. runCodify() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
A test-plan.md with TC rows. Anthropic API mocked to return spec.ts content.
Expected outcome
  • ✓spec.ts written with form.requestSubmit() for React form submissions and afterEach hooks that save screenshot evidence.
TRUNK-55 codify: --max-spend gate
P1 #
R-IDs: R-25 R-34
General
Steps
  1. runCodify() called.
Pre-condition (Given)
A test-plan.md. --max-spend set to a value less than the estimated cost.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Exit code 1.
  • ✓No API call made.
  • ✓Estimated cost displayed.
TRUNK-56 enrich: append-only marker boundaries
P0 #
R-IDs: R-26 R-33
General
Steps
  1. runEnrich() is called twice.
Pre-condition (Given)
A test-plan.md with existing human-edited content outside enrichment marker blocks.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Human-edited content outside markers is byte-identical after both runs.
  • ✓Content inside markers replaced (not doubled).
TRUNK-57 enrich: [VERIFY] markers on enrichment blocks
P1 #
R-IDs: R-26 R-29
General
Steps
  1. runEnrich() called.
Pre-condition (Given)
A test-plan.md. Anthropic API mocked to return enrichment content.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Each enrichment block starts with a [VERIFY] marker.
TRUNK-58 batch-plan: parallel pipeline with failure isolation
P0 #
R-IDs: R-27 R-34
General
Steps
  1. runBatchPlan() called.
Pre-condition (Given)
Multiple pages queued. One page's discover/plan step is mocked to throw.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Failed page is reported but does not abort remaining pages.
  • ✓Successful pages complete.
TRUNK-59 sca-sanitize: XSS payload stripped from SCA markdown
P0 #
R-IDs: R-08 R-09
Security — Injection
Steps
  1. The sanitize function is called on each payload.
Pre-condition (Given)
SCA markdown strings containing ``, `onerror=`, and `
Expected outcome
  • ✓Output contains no script tags, no on* event handlers, no file:// iframe src.
TRUNK-60 graph: buildGraph and Graph structure
P1 #
R-IDs: R-07
Dashboard
Steps
  1. buildGraph() is called.
Pre-condition (Given)
A set of requirement R-IDs and their dependency edges.
Expected outcome
  • ✓Returns a Graph object with correct nodes and edges.
  • ✓graph.nodes has one entry per R-ID.
Generated by TrunkNuX v0.2.0-alpha  ·  2026-04-27T11:04:48.563Z  ·  60 test cases
